agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"E-commerce platform managing the product catalog, shopping cart, checkout, promotions, and post-purchase lifecycle. The domain enforces inventory integrity at checkout time (not cart time), limits promotional stacking, ensures order immutability after payment, and governs refund eligibility windows.","bounded_context":"Order & Checkout bounded context, with touches into Inventory, Promotions, and Returns contexts.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Cart","definition":"A transient collection of items a customer intends to purchase. Holding items in a cart does NOT reserve inventory."},{"term":"Inventory Reservation","definition":"A time-limited hold on inventory quantity that begins when checkout starts and is released if payment is not completed within the reservation window."},{"term":"Reservation Window","definition":"The 15-minute period after checkout begins during which inventory is held for the customer. Expiry releases the reservation."},{"term":"Active Promotion","definition":"A currently-in-effect price reduction applied to a product at the catalog level. A product may have at most one active promotion at any time."},{"term":"Discount Code","definition":"A customer-supplied code that applies a discount at the order level, distinct from catalog-level promotional pricing."},{"term":"Discount Mechanism","definition":"Any single source of price reduction on an order — either a discount code OR promotional pricing, never both simultaneously."},{"term":"Payment Confirmed","definition":"The irreversible state transition indicating funds have been successfully captured. After this point the order is immutable."},{"term":"Zero Inventory","definition":"A product whose available quantity is zero. Such products may remain listed (visible) but are not purchasable."},{"term":"Refund Window","definition":"The 30-day period following delivery during which a customer may request a refund."},{"term":"Delivery Date","definition":"The date the order is recorded as delivered, which starts the refund eligibility window."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"Adding items to the cart does not reserve inventory. Inventory is only reserved when checkout begins.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"If cart additions reserved inventory, phantom demand would corrupt stock levels system-wide. Reservation at checkout is a data-integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: After adding an item to cart, the product's available inventory count is unchanged."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"If payment is not completed within 15 minutes of checkout initiation, the inventory reservation is released.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 15-minute window is a business policy. It could be changed to 10 or 20 minutes by a product decision without corrupting system state.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: A reservation created at time T is released at T+15 minutes if no payment has been confirmed."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"A product may have at most one active promotion at any given time.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Multiple simultaneous promotions on the same product would create ambiguous pricing, violating price integrity. The system cannot determine which price to display.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: For any product, COUNT(active promotions) <= 1 at all times."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Discount codes cannot be combined with promotional pricing. Only one discount mechanism applies per order line.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Stacking discounts without explicit rules leads to unbounded price reductions and revenue integrity violations. This is a financial integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: For any order, if a product has an active promotion applied, no discount code may also apply to that line item, and vice versa."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Orders cannot be modified after payment is confirmed.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Post-payment mutation would break financial reconciliation, audit trails, and fulfillment integrity. This is a state-machine invariant.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: Any mutation attempt on an order in 'payment_confirmed' or later state is rejected."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"A product with zero available inventory cannot be purchased, even if it remains listed in the catalog.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Selling non-existent stock creates fulfillment obligations that cannot be met. This is a physical constraint.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: Checkout initiation for a product with available_inventory == 0 is rejected."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"Refund requests must be submitted within 30 days of the recorded delivery date.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 30-day window is a customer-service policy that could be extended or shortened by business decision without corrupting system state.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: A refund request submitted on delivery_date + 31 days is rejected. A request on delivery_date + 30 days is accepted."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A customer has items in their cart","when":"The customer views their cart but has not started checkout","then":"The product's available inventory remains unchanged and other customers can still purchase the same items"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A customer initiates checkout for a product with available inventory > 0","when":"Checkout begins","then":"The requested quantity is reserved (decremented from available inventory) and a 15-minute reservation timer starts"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A customer has an active checkout reservation","when":"15 minutes elapse without payment confirmation","then":"The reservation is released, the reserved quantity is returned to available inventory, and the checkout session is invalidated"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A customer completes payment within the 15-minute window","when":"Payment is confirmed","then":"The reservation becomes a permanent allocation, the order transitions to an immutable confirmed state, and the timer is cancelled"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A product already has one active promotion","when":"An attempt is made to add a second promotion to the same product","then":"The system rejects the second promotion, preserving the existing one"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A customer applies a discount code to an order containing a product with an active promotion","when":"The system evaluates applicable discounts","then":"Only one discount mechanism is applied to each line item — the system does not stack the discount code on top of promotional pricing"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"An order has reached 'payment confirmed' status","when":"Any modification to the order is attempted (item changes, quantity changes, address changes)","then":"The modification is rejected and the order remains unchanged"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A product is listed in the catalog with available inventory equal to zero","when":"A customer attempts to add it to checkout","then":"The purchase is blocked with a clear out-of-stock indication, even though the product listing remains visible"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"An order was delivered 29 days ago","when":"The customer requests a refund","then":"The refund request is accepted for processing"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"An order was delivered 31 days ago","when":"The customer requests a refund","then":"The refund request is rejected as outside the eligible window"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"Multiple customers attempt to purchase the last unit of a product simultaneously","when":"Both initiate checkout at nearly the same time","then":"Only one customer successfully reserves the item; the other receives an out-of-stock rejection"},{"id":"AC-12","given":"A checkout reservation has just expired for a product","when":"Another customer immediately initiates checkout for that same product","then":"The newly-available inventory is reservable by the second customer"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Concurrent checkout for last item","expected_behavior":"Exactly one reservation succeeds. The losing request is rejected with an out-of-stock error. Requires atomic inventory decrement."},{"scenario":"Payment confirmation arrives at exactly the 15-minute boundary","expected_behavior":"The system must define a clear tiebreaker: either the reservation is valid at T+15:00.000 and invalid at T+15:00.001, or use strict less-than. Race condition must be handled atomically."},{"scenario":"Discount code applied to a mixed cart (some items on promotion, some not)","expected_behavior":"Per BR-4, promotional items retain their promotional price; the discount code applies only to non-promoted items. This interpretation needs business confirmation (see escalation)."},{"scenario":"Promotion activated on a product while a customer has it in checkout with a discount code","expected_behavior":"The discount mechanism was locked at checkout initiation. Mid-checkout promotion changes should not alter the pricing already presented to the customer."},{"scenario":"Product inventory drops to zero between cart addition and checkout initiation","expected_behavior":"Checkout is blocked per BR-6. The cart may show the item but checkout validation rejects it."},{"scenario":"Refund requested for an order not yet marked as delivered","expected_behavior":"The 30-day window has not started. The system should either reject (not yet eligible) or hold the request. Spec does not clarify — see escalation."},{"scenario":"Partial refund on a multi-item order where some items are past 30 days","expected_behavior":"Each line item's refund eligibility should be evaluated against the delivery date. If all items share one delivery date, it is straightforward; if delivered in multiple shipments, each has its own window."},{"scenario":"Customer abandons checkout at 14 minutes, then re-initiates at 14:30","expected_behavior":"If the original session is still alive, the reservation persists. If the customer starts a new checkout, a new reservation with a fresh 15-minute timer should be created."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Inventory reservation at checkout (not cart) maximizes sell-through by preventing phantom holds. The single-discount-mechanism rule protects margins. The 30-day refund window bounds return liability.","operational":"The 15-minute reservation window requires a reliable timer/scheduler mechanism. Concurrent checkout for scarce inventory requires atomic operations. Promotion uniqueness per product simplifies pricing logic.","user":"Customers see products listed that may be out of stock, which could cause frustration. The inability to combine discounts may reduce perceived value. The 15-minute checkout window creates urgency that may not suit all customers.","risk":"Race conditions on inventory reservation are the highest technical risk. Discount conflict resolution logic (which mechanism wins) is underspecified and could lead to customer disputes. The exact-moment-of-delivery ambiguity for refund windows could create support escalations.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Cannot stack discounts, must complete checkout within 15 minutes, refunds limited to 30 days post-delivery. Overall fair but discount limitation may cause friction."},{"group":"Product/Merchandising Team","impact":"Must manage one-promotion-per-product constraint. Cannot run overlapping campaigns on the same SKU."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Will handle disputes around discount conflicts, borderline refund window cases, and out-of-stock frustrations on listed products."},{"group":"Finance/Accounting","impact":"Order immutability post-payment simplifies reconciliation. Single discount mechanism per order simplifies revenue accounting."},{"group":"Warehouse/Fulfillment","impact":"Reservation-based inventory ensures only purchasable quantities are committed. Reduces oversell risk."}]},"confidence":{"level":55,"reasoning":"The core rules are explicitly stated and internally consistent — no contradictions detected. However, several operationally critical details are ambiguous: which discount mechanism takes priority when both could apply, how mixed carts are handled, and the refund window boundary for undelivered orders. These ambiguities could lead to different implementations depending on interpretation.","high_confidence_areas":["Inventory is not reserved at cart time — clear and unambiguous","15-minute reservation timeout — explicit policy","One active promotion per product — clear invariant","Order immutability after payment — explicit state constraint","Zero-inventory blocks purchase — clear physical constraint","30-day refund window from delivery — clear policy"],"low_confidence_areas":["Refund eligibility for orders with multiple shipments and different delivery dates","Behavior when payment gateway response arrives exactly at the timeout boundary","Whether abandoned checkout means only timeout or also explicit user cancellation"],"assumptions":["The 30-day refund window uses calendar days, not business days","Delivery date refers to a system-recorded event, not customer self-report","Inventory reservation is per-unit (quantity-based), not per-SKU binary lock","The 15-minute timer starts from checkout initiation, not from the last user interaction"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"When a customer applies a discount code to an order containing items with active promotional pricing, the spec says only one discount mechanism applies but does not specify WHICH mechanism takes priority. Does the promotional price always win? Does the discount code always win? Does the customer choose? Does the system pick the better deal for the customer?","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Product/Merchandising Team","Finance/Accounting","Customer Support"],"options":["Promotional pricing always takes precedence over discount codes","Discount codes always take precedence over promotional pricing","System automatically applies whichever gives the customer the greater discount","Customer can choose which discount mechanism to apply"],"recommendation":"Option C (best-deal-for-customer) is most customer-friendly and reduces support disputes, but has margin implications. Recommend business stakeholders explicitly decide and document the priority rule."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The spec states refunds must be requested within 30 days of delivery, but does not address what happens if a refund is requested before the order is marked as delivered (e.g., in-transit orders). Is the customer eligible, ineligible, or should the request be queued?","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Customer Support"],"options":["Refund requests are only accepted for orders in 'delivered' status — pre-delivery requests are rejected","Refund requests are accepted at any post-payment state but the 30-day window only starts at delivery","A separate return/cancellation policy governs pre-delivery refunds"],"recommendation":"Option B is the most pragmatic — accept the request, but the 30-day clock does not start until delivery. Pre-delivery refunds should follow a cancellation policy, which is likely a separate business rule not yet specified."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"For mixed carts containing both promoted and non-promoted items, it is unclear whether the discount code restriction applies at the order level (entire order gets one mechanism) or at the line-item level (each line independently gets one mechanism). The spec says 'per order' but practical implementation likely needs line-item granularity.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Product/Merchandising Team","Finance/Accounting"],"options":["Order-level: if ANY item has a promotion, no discount code can apply to the entire order","Line-item-level: discount code applies to non-promoted items; promoted items keep their promotional price","Customer must choose one mechanism for the whole order"],"recommendation":"Option B (line-item level) is industry standard and maximizes customer value without violating the no-stacking rule. Recommend confirming with product team."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"E-commerce platform managing the product catalog, shopping cart, checkout, promotions, and post-purchase lifecycle. The domain enforces inventory integrity at checkout time (not cart time), limits promotional stacking, ensures order immutability after payment, and governs refund eligibility windows.","bounded_context":"Order & Checkout bounded context, with touches into Inventory, Promotions, and Returns contexts.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Cart","de

content.thinking:
Let me analyze this e-commerce domain scenario carefully.

The rules are clear and well-defined. Let me classify each rule and check for any issues.

Rules:
1. Cart doesn't reserve inventory - reservation happens at checkout
2. 15-minute checkout timeout releases reservation
3. Max one active promotion per product
4. Discount codes can't combine with promotional pricing - one discount mechanism per order
5. Orders immutable after payment confirmed
6. Zero inventory = can't purchase even if listed
7. Refunds within 30 days of delivery

These are all fairly clear. No contradictions. 

I'm spotting some ambiguities worth noting: the discount priority rule doesn't specify which mechanism wins if both apply, the checkout abandonment logic differs between payment failures and timeouts, and the refund window's delivery date definition is unclear. These are operational details rather than fundamental contradictions, so I'd flag this as an ambiguity issue with moderate confidence around 55% since the core rules hold up but some implementation specifics need clarification.
